results.question: Explain the difference between law and grace.
What is the meaning of the 2Sons allegory?
How do we distinguish between the 2Voices?
What does it mean to be LukeWarm?
What is the relationship between the kingdom of God and the believer?
results.text:
> **Thinking Process:**

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The kingdom of God is not a place to enter. It is a Person to be with. The believer is not under the kingdom. The believer is in Christ, and Christ is the kingdom. For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost (Rom 14:17). This is not a realm of law. This is the realm of grace.

- kingdom of heaven ← allegory for law (cOLD covenant)  
- kingdom of God → allegory for grace (new covenant)  
- The believer is translated from the power of darkness into the kingdom of God's dear Son (Col 1:13).  

The kingdom of God is "within you" (Lk 17:21). This is only innerstood after grace is mentioned in Luke 2:40. Before grace, the kingdom of heaven was preached by John the Baptist (violent law prophet). After John beheaded, JC began preaching the kingdom of God (Mk 1:1-15; Lk 16:16). This is the end of law and prophets mode, the begin of grace and truth mode. [^1]

The believer does not seek the kingdom of heaven. The believer seeks first the kingdom of God (Mt 6:33). Why? Because the kingdom of heaven is like leaven (Mt 13:33). Leaven is a type of sin and law. The kingdom of God is like a grain of mustard seed (Mt 13:31). Mustard seed is faith. Faith is not of law. Faith is of grace. [^2]

The believer's relationship to the kingdom of God is not of f-laws. It is of knowing. Knowing is what saves us (Jn 8:32). Not believing. Believing is still a work. Knowing is a gift. The kingdom of God is given to the believer. It is not earned. It is not kept by law. It is kept by grace. [^3]

The kingdom of God is within you. This means the believer is the temple of the Holy Ghost. The kingdom is not external. It is internal. It is not a future hope. It is a present reality. The believer is already seated in heavenly places in Christ Jesus (Eph 2:6). This is the kingdom of God. [^4]

LukeWarm mode is mixing law and grace. This is the error of the Galatians. They began in the Spirit but ended in the flesh (Gal 3:3). The believer must ear what the Spirit saith unto the churches: law is not of faith (Gal 3:12). The kingdom of God is not of law. It is of grace. [^5]

The believer is not under the kingdom. The believer is with the kingdom. The kingdom is within. The believer is in Christ. Christ is the head. The believer is the body. The head and body are one. This is the mystery of the kingdom of God. [^6]

**Learn More:**  
- [The Kingdom of Heaven vs Kingdom of God](https://godshew.org/pages/kingdoms)  
- [Post 2670: Kingdom Allegories](https://godshew.org/pages/post_2670)  
- [Post 2603: Kingdom of God Within You](https://godshew.org/pages/post_2603)  
- [Post 0931: Kingdom Occurrences](https://godshew.org/pages/post_0931)  
- [Post 1822: Kingdom Difference](https://godshew.org/pages/post_1822)  
- [Post 1734: Kingdom of God Within](https://godshew.org/pages/post_1734)  
- [Shewbread 7: Two Worlds](https://godshew.org/pages/shewbread7)  

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: Post 2670
1.snippet: it kinda makes the 9 'Blessed's mentioned in Mt 5 to be law blessed, which can still be both blessed & cursed by law is both good & evil.

2.title: Post 2603
2.snippet: For the kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven: Mt 13:33.

3.title: Post 0931
3.snippet: JC doesn't even BEGIN to preach gospel of the kingdom of God UNTIL John the Baptist beheaded = end of the law & the prophets.

4.title: Kingdoms Study
4.snippet: The 'kingdom of heaven' only occurs in Matthew, wherein JC Mt 6:33 said: But seek ye first the 'kingdom of God'.

5.title: Post 1822
5.snippet: Seems 'kingdom of heaven' phrase was as allegory for law and 'kingdom of God' is allegory for grace.

6.title: Post 1734
6.snippet: Of twain, only the 'kingdom of God' is said to be 'within you'.

7.title: Shewbread 7
7.snippet: JC said God said, speaking to Pilate: my 'kingdom' is not of 'this world'.
